common.errorRetry: Check your connection settings and try again.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: API accuracy, test coverage and cost across your services.
overview.execTail: {{findings}} open findings, {{defects}} open defects. Estimated AI spend this environment is ${{spend}}.
overview.setupBody: {{names}} need attention before Veritas can run.
overview.setupBodyMore: {{names}} and {{count}} more need attention before Veritas can run.
overview.pipelineSubtitle: What exists at each stage, per service — strategy through codegen.
overview.noValidationsBody: Validate a service to see its findings and a management report here.

onboarding.pageSubtitle: Three quick steps and you can validate a contract, manage tests and generate automation — all from here.
onboarding.getSetUpSubtitle: Everything is configured in the app — no terminal, no env vars.
onboarding.step1Body: Veritas uses Copilot as its reasoning engine. Sign in once on the Settings page.
onboarding.step2Body: Add your Bitbucket base URL + token so Veritas can discover and clone your repos.
onboarding.step3Body: Optional, but needed for defects, test management and coverage. Test each connection live in Settings.
onboarding.readyBody: Run your first contract validation to see findings and an executive report.
onboarding.notReadyBody: Finish the steps above, then run your first validation.

glossary.pageSubtitle: What the words on the report and the findings list actually mean — in plain language.
glossary.intro: Veritas compares what a service's <1>code actually does</1> with what its <3>API contract promises</3>, and lists every difference as a <5>finding</5>. The terms below explain how those findings are labelled so you can judge them without reading the code.
glossary.bottomLineSubtitle: The one-glance verdict at the top of every management report.
glossary.proceedBody: The contract matches the code closely enough to release. No release-blocking differences were found.
glossary.holdBody: The service works, but the contract has gaps worth correcting before you publish it. Nothing here blocks a release on its own.
glossary.doNotReleaseBody: At least one release-blocking difference was found — something a consumer relies on is wrong or missing. Fix these before shipping.
glossary.severitySubtitle: How much a single difference matters. Higher severity = more impact on the people calling the API.
glossary.blockerBody: Will break callers in production. Must be fixed before release.
glossary.criticalBody: A serious mismatch that is very likely to cause failures or confusion. Fix before release.
glossary.majorBody: A real gap worth correcting — the contract is misleading, but most callers will still work.
glossary.minorBody: A small inaccuracy or polish item. Safe to defer.
glossary.infoBody: An observation, not a defect. Nothing to fix.
glossary.confidenceSubtitle: How sure Veritas is that a finding is real. Use it to decide what to double-check.
glossary.confidenceHighBody: Proven directly from the code or the contract. Safe to act on as-is.
glossary.confidenceMediumBody: Well-supported, but worth a quick look before raising a defect.
glossary.confidenceLowBody: A reasonable suspicion that needs a human to confirm. A <1>high-severity finding marked low confidence</1> is flagged with a warning icon — verify it before you treat it as a blocker.
glossary.analysisAreaSubtitle: Which part of the API a finding is about. Veritas checks six areas; you never need the internal codes.
glossary.layerBlurbL1: Is the contract itself well-formed and structurally valid?
glossary.layerBlurbL2: Does the contract list every endpoint the code actually exposes — and nothing it doesn't?
glossary.layerBlurbL3: Are the endpoints, parameters and responses documented clearly enough to use?
glossary.layerBlurbL4: Do the request and response shapes in the contract match what the code really sends and accepts?
glossary.layerBlurbL5: Does the API follow sound design conventions (naming, status codes, consistency)?
glossary.layerBlurbL6: Is there enough in the contract to derive a solid set of tests from it?
glossary.reviewStatusSubtitle: What you've decided about a finding. Your decision is recorded with your name and the time.
glossary.acceptedBody: A real difference that matches the code. It stays in the management report and can be raised as a defect.
glossary.rejectedBody: Not applicable to this service. It's removed from the report and won't be raised as a defect. You can change this later.
glossary.defectRaisedBody: A Jira defect has been created for this finding and is now tracked there.
glossary.reportMetricsSubtitle: The numbers on the management report.
glossary.contractFidelityBody: A 0–100 score for how faithfully the published contract describes the real service. Higher is better; the release gate passes at 90 or above.
glossary.analysisCoverageBody: How much of the service Veritas was able to examine. <1>Full</1> means every source it needed was available; <3>Partial</3> means something (a security source, an exception handler, a DTO) wasn't supplied, so some checks were skipped.
glossary.estAnalysisCostBody: The estimated cost of the AI calls used for this validation. Most checks are done without AI; it's used only where a deterministic check isn't enough.

costs.pageSubtitle: Estimated Copilot spend in this environment, broken down by skill.
costs.emptyBody: Run a skill that calls the LLM and its cost will be tracked here.

defects.title: Defects
defects.subtitle: Jira defects raised from contract findings, with live status.
defects.emptyBody: Defects you raise from findings will appear here, and Veritas will keep their Jira status in sync.

gates.title: Approval gates
gates.subtitle: Outward actions wait here for a human decision — nothing is written before you approve.
gates.emptyBody: When a skill needs approval before writing to Jira, Xray or Git, it will appear here.

repos.pageSubtitle: Find a service by its app-id, then check whether its API spec matches the code.
repos.appIdHint: Your project key in Bitbucket (ask your admin if unsure), e.g. CIAM or APP7571. This is not the repository name.
repos.emptyFoundBody: No repositories matched that app-id, or your token cannot access them. Check the app-id and your Bitbucket token in Settings.
repos.emptySearchBody: Enter an app-id above to list the repositories your Bitbucket token can access.
repos.specRepoPathHint: Path to the OpenAPI/Swagger file, relative to the repo root.
repos.specLiveDocsHint: A running endpoint that serves the OpenAPI JSON.
repos.specConfluenceHint: Paste the page URL or just its numeric ID — the page must hold the OpenAPI/Swagger spec.
repos.modalIntroPrefix: Veritas reads the API endpoints and data types defined in
repos.modalIntroSuffix: and compares them to the spec below. Read-only — nothing in the repository is changed. This usually takes 2–5 minutes.
repos.copilotWarnPrefix: Not connected to GitHub Copilot — the
repos.copilotWarnSuffix: step will be skipped. You'll still get the full deterministic contract findings and fidelity score.
repos.thoroughnessHint: How deeply the AI reviews the findings — trades cost for rigour.
repos.thoroughnessDeep: Deep — strongest model, for a release gate
repos.aiHintShort: The AI is reviewing the findings and generating a corrected spec — usually a minute or two.
repos.aiHintMedium: Still generating the corrected spec — larger contracts take a few minutes.
repos.aiHintLong: This is a large contract — the AI is still working ({{elapsed}}). You can keep waiting or run it in the background.
repos.scanStoppedNote: The scan stopped before it finished. Nothing was written to the repo.
repos.scanStaticNote: Veritas runs entirely from static analysis — your app is never started. Keep watching, or run it in the background.
repos.adjustAndRetry: Adjust the inputs and try again

reviews.noDetail: No detail captured for this review.
reviews.pageSubtitle: Score existing Xray tests against the ISTQB Test-Analyst rubric (C1–C6).
reviews.newReviewSubtitle: Find tests by JQL, pick which to review, optionally write the verdict back to Jira.
reviews.jqlHint: Which Xray tests, e.g. project = CIAM AND issuetype = Test — load to pick a subset, or review all
reviews.toastNoCandidates: The JQL returned no Xray tests.
reviews.applyLabel: Apply the corrected steps back to Xray (gated write)
reviews.recentSubtitle: Previously scored tests — reopen a verdict any time.
reviews.emptyNoReviewBody: Run a review above to see per-test verdicts and scores.
reviews.emptyNoMatchBody: The JQL returned no Xray tests to review.
